# UC-17.1.46 · Cisco Identity Intelligence (CII) Risk-Score Drift and Adaptive-Auth Triggering

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Cisco's identity-risk service combines signals from multiple security tools and gives each user a risk score. We watch the link to that service — and ring the bell if the link breaks or if it triggers extra security checks for users we should know about.*

---

## Description

Tracks Cisco Identity Intelligence integration health (feed import, cert validity) and audits cases where CII risk-score elevation triggered adaptive auth (step-up MFA, additional verification, quarantine).

## Value

CII is the consolidation point for cross-platform identity risk; when the integration breaks, ISE adaptive-auth decisions revert to ISE-only signals. Auditing the CII-triggered actions provides evidence the cross-source enrichment is materially driving access decisions.

## Implementation

Confirm *Identity Intelligence* category at INFO. Deploy CII REST API poller every 30 min. Schedule the search every 15 min, search window `-1h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Misconfigure CII API credentials. ISE emits 82502 on next sync attempt. UC-17.1.46 fires.

### Negative scenario

Restore credentials. Sync succeeds. Alert clears.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cisco Identity Intelligence subscription active.
- ISE 3.3+ with CII integration configured.
- `cii_high_risk_actions.csv` lookup of accepted adaptive-auth actions.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Identity Intelligence* category at INFO. Deploy CII REST API poller.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 15 min.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — CII Adaptive Auth":
- Single value: triggered actions.
- Trend: avg risk score.
- Top-N: triggered identities.

Alerting: medium PagerDuty on cert_error or sustained feed_failures.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **Feed failures every 30 min** — API credential rotation or token expiry.
- **No 82500 events** — Category at WARN; lower to INFO.
- **Triggered_actions = 0 over 30d** — CII feed working but ISE adaptive-auth policy not consuming the score; review policy conditions.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (82500, 82501, 82502, 82503))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:cii")
| stats count, latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode=="82502")) as feed_failures, count(eval(MessageCode=="82503")) as cert_errors, count(eval(MessageCode=="82501")) as triggered_actions by endpoint_user
| where feed_failures > 0 OR cert_errors > 0 OR triggered_actions > 0
| sort - triggered_actions
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: triggered actions last 24h. (2) Trend: avg risk-score per identity over 30d. (3) Top-N: identities with most triggered actions.

## Known False Positives

- **CII service maintenance** — Cisco-side CII maintenance briefly drops feed; cross-reference Cisco trust-center status.
- **Initial onboarding** — First days of CII deployment, score baselines are stabilising; many adaptive-auth triggers may fire as the model calibrates.

## References

- [Cisco Identity Intelligence (CII) Overview](https://www.cisco.com/site/us/en/products/security/)
- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Identity Intelligence Integration](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
